title: Topology Coupling Artifact
description: Flat per-module view of Martin's package metrics, optimized for fitness-rule consumption. Written to .topology/metrics/coupling.json. This is a denormalized projection of the martin block in modules.json — the same data, flat at the module level, with explicit long-form field names. Consumers that want the full module record should read modules.json; consumers that only need coupling governance read coupling.json.

id.description: Stable unique module identifier, matching Module.id in modules.json.

path.description: File or directory path, relative to the analysis root. Included so fitness rules can filter by path glob without a join against modules.json.

afferent_coupling.description: Ca: number of modules that depend on this module. Martin (1994). Lower is more independent.

efferent_coupling.description: Ce: number of modules this module depends on. Martin (1994). Higher means more fan-out / more things can break this module.

instability.description: I = Ce / (Ca + Ce). 0 = maximally stable (few reasons to change), 1 = maximally unstable. Defaults to 0.5 when Ca+Ce=0.

abstractness.description: A = abstract_types / total_types. Defaults to 0 when total_types=0.

distance_from_main_sequence.description: D = |A + I - 1|. 0 = on the main sequence (A + I = 1, balanced between abstract-and-stable or concrete-and-unstable). 1 = zone of pain (concrete and stable, hard to change) or zone of uselessness (abstract and unstable, nobody uses it).
